title: Choose a sort signal

publish.description: Understand the seven sort signals, the sales window, and the update cadence on a collection's Setup tab.

intro: A sort rule is built from three choices on the Setup tab: which signal ranks your products, how far back the sales window looks, and how often qSortBy re-sorts. This guide explains all three so you can dial in exactly the ordering you want.

steps.heading: Pick the ranking signal
steps.body: Left menu → **Collections** → open a collection → the **Setup** tab. Under **Sort products by**, choose one of seven signals: **Best sellers** (units sold), **Highest CTR** (click-through rate), **Highest revenue** (sales value), **Newest** (recently added), **Most inventory** (stock on hand), **Trending** (recent momentum), or **Manual pinning** (a hand-set order). Each ranks the same products differently.

els.text: Left menu → Collections → open a collection → the Setup tab, then pick a signal under Sort products by.

notes.text: Use Trending to surface fast-rising products, or Best sellers for a steadier, proven order.

steps.heading: Set the sales window
steps.body: The **Use sales from** window controls how far back qSortBy looks when measuring a signal. A short window reacts quickly to what's selling right now; a longer window smooths out day-to-day swings and favours consistent performers.

els.text: A short window reacts to what's hot now; a longer window rewards steady sellers.

steps.heading: Choose the update cadence
steps.body: Decide how often the collection re-sorts. Continuous updates re-rank as sales come in for the freshest order, while an hourly or daily cadence gives a steadier storefront that changes less often. Pick the rhythm that suits your catalogue.

els.text: Re-sort continuously for the freshest order, or on a daily/hourly schedule for a steadier storefront.

steps.heading: Confirm it in plain words
steps.body: The **In plain words** panel restates your signal, window, and cadence as one readable sentence — a quick way to confirm the rule does what you intend before you save it.

els.text: The plain-words summary spells out your whole rule in one sentence.

outro: Signal, sales window, and cadence together define how a collection sorts. Adjust any of the three and the In plain words summary updates so you always know exactly what qSortBy will do.
